contents:
(** * Homogeneous squares *)

(** Squares are fillers in diagrams of the following form:

<<
        a00 ----p0i---- a01
         |               |
         |         >     |
        pi0      =     pi1
         |     =         |
         |               |
        a10-----p1i-----a11
>>

Indexing of points in a square follows the convention for matrices, first a row index, then a column index. Unless stated otherwise, paths are oriented in the direction of increasing index i (or x, etc). The stylized 2-path on the antidiagonal goes from [pi0 @ p1i] to [p0i @ pi1], complying with the definition of [equiv_sq_path] below. *)

(** Contents:

  * Definition of [PathSquare] and basic properties
  * Degenerate [PathSquare]s as paths between paths
  * Flipping squares horizontally and vertically
  * [PathSquare] transpose
  * [PathSquare] inverse
  * [PathSquare] rotations
  * Edge rewriting
  * Concatenation and groupoid laws
  * Kan fillers and uniqueness, induction principles requiring only one free edge
  * Interchange law
  * Squares arising from [ap]
  * [PathSquare]s respect products
*)

(** Definition of [PathSquare left right up down]. *)

Cumulative Inductive PathSquare {A} : forall a00 {a10 a01 a11 : A},
  a00 = a10 -> a01 = a11 -> a00 = a01 -> a10 = a11 -> Type
  := sq_id : forall {x : A},
    PathSquare x 1 1 1 1.

(** TODO: ": rename" is needed because the default names changed in Rocq 9.2.0.  When the minimum supported version is >= 9.2.0, the ": rename" can be removed. *)
(** register=no: otherwise the scheme confuses the make_equiv tactic. When the minimum supported version is >= 9.2.0, the warning can be re-enabled. *)
#[warnings="-unsupported-attributes",register=no] Scheme PathSquare_ind := Induction for PathSquare Sort Type.
#[warnings="-unsupported-attributes",register=no] Scheme PathSquare_rec := Minimality for PathSquare Sort Type.
(** [PathSquare_ind] is an equivalence, similar to how [paths_ind] is. *)

Instance isequiv_PathSquare_ind `{Funext} {A}
  (P : forall (a00 a10 a01 a11 : A) (p : a00 = a10) (p0 : a01 = a11)
    (p1 : a00 = a01) (p2 : a10 = a11),
    PathSquare p p0 p1 p2 -> Type) : IsEquiv (PathSquare_ind P).

goals.conclusion: IsEquiv (PathSquare_ind P)

hypotheses.type:
forall (a00 a10 a01 a11 : A) (p : a00 = a10) (p0 : a01 = a11)
(p1 : a00 = a01) (p2 : a10 = a11), PathSquare p p0 p1 p2 -> Type

goals.conclusion: IsEquiv (PathSquare_ind P)

hypotheses.type:
forall (a00 a10 a01 a11 : A) (p : a00 = a10) (p0 : a01 = a11)
(p1 : a00 = a01) (p2 : a10 = a11), PathSquare p p0 p1 p2 -> Type

goals.conclusion:
(forall (a00 a10 a01 a11 : A) (p : a00 = a10) (p0 : a01 = a11)
 (p1 : a00 = a01) (p2 : a10 = a11) (p3 : PathSquare p p0 p1 p2),
 P a00 a10 a01 a11 p p0 p1 p2 p3) ->
forall x : A, P x x x x 1 1 1 1 1%square

hypotheses.type:
forall (a00 a10 a01 a11 : A) (p : a00 = a10) (p0 : a01 = a11)
(p1 : a00 = a01) (p2 : a10 = a11), PathSquare p p0 p1 p2 -> Type

goals.conclusion: PathSquare_ind P o ?g == idmap

hypotheses.type:
forall (a00 a10 a01 a11 : A) (p : a00 = a10) (p0 : a01 = a11)
(p1 : a00 = a01) (p2 : a10 = a11), PathSquare p p0 p1 p2 -> Type

goals.conclusion: ?g o PathSquare_ind P == idmap

hypotheses.type:
forall (a00 a10 a01 a11 : A) (p : a00 = a10) (p0 : a01 = a11)
(p1 : a00 = a01) (p2 : a10 = a11), PathSquare p p0 p1 p2 -> Type

goals.conclusion:
PathSquare_ind P
o (fun
     (X : forall (a00 a10 a01 a11 : A) (p : a00 = a10) 
          (p0 : a01 = a11) (p1 : a00 = a01) (p2 : a10 = a11)
          (p3 : PathSquare p p0 p1 p2), P a00 a10 a01 a11 p p0 p1 p2 p3)
     (x : A) =>
   X x x x x 1 1 1 1 1%square) ==
idmap

hypotheses.type:
forall (a00 a10 a01 a11 : A) (p : a00 = a10) (p0 : a01 = a11)
(p1 : a00 = a01) (p2 : a10 = a11), PathSquare p p0 p1 p2 -> Type

goals.conclusion:
(fun
   (X : forall (a00 a10 a01 a11 : A) (p : a00 = a10) 
        (p0 : a01 = a11) (p1 : a00 = a01) (p2 : a10 = a11)
        (p3 : PathSquare p p0 p1 p2), P a00 a10 a01 a11 p p0 p1 p2 p3)
   (x : A) =>
 X x x x x 1 1 1 1 1%square)
o PathSquare_ind P == idmap

hypotheses.type:
forall (a00 a10 a01 a11 : A) (p : a00 = a10) (p0 : a01 = a11)
(p1 : a00 = a01) (p2 : a10 = a11), PathSquare p p0 p1 p2 -> Type

goals.conclusion:
PathSquare_ind P
o (fun
     (X : forall (a00 a10 a01 a11 : A) (p : a00 = a10) 
          (p0 : a01 = a11) (p1 : a00 = a01) (p2 : a10 = a11)
          (p3 : PathSquare p p0 p1 p2), P a00 a10 a01 a11 p p0 p1 p2 p3)
     (x : A) =>
   X x x x x 1 1 1 1 1%square) ==
idmap

hypotheses.type:
forall (a00 a10 a01 a11 : A) (p : a00 = a10) (p0 : a01 = a11)
(p1 : a00 = a01) (p2 : a10 = a11), PathSquare p p0 p1 p2 -> Type

goals.conclusion: @PathSquare_ind A P (fun x0 : A => x x0 x0 x0 x0 1 1 1 1 1%square) = x

forall (a00 a10 a01 a11 : A) (p : a00 = a10) (p0 : a01 = a11)
(p1 : a00 = a01) (p2 : a10 = a11), PathSquare p p0 p1 p2 -> Type

(p1 : a00 = a01) (p2 : a10 = a11) (p3 : PathSquare p p0 p1 p2),
P a00 a10 a01 a11 p p0 p1 p2 p3

PathSquare_ind P (fun x8 : A => x x8 x8 x8 x8 1 1 1 1 1%square) =

PathSquare_ind P (fun x8 : A => x x8 x8 x8 x8 1 1 1 1 1%square) ==

forall (a00 a10 a01 a11 : A) (p : a00 = a10) (p0 : a01 = a11)
(p1 : a00 = a01) (p2 : a10 = a11), PathSquare p p0 p1 p2 -> Type

goals.conclusion: px0 @ p1x = p0x @ px1 <~> PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x
px0 @ p1x = p0x @ px1 <~> PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x
px0 @ p1x = p0x @ px1 -> PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x
IsEquiv ?equiv_fun
px0 @ p1x = p0x @ px1 -> PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x
px0 @ 1 = 1 @ px1 -> PathSquare px0 px1 1 1
PathSquare px0 px1 1 1
px0 @ 1 = px1 -> PathSquare px0 px1 1 1
PathSquare px0 px1 1 1
PathSquare 1 (1 @ 1) 1 1

IsEquiv
  (match
     p0x as p in (_ = a)
     return
       (forall px2 : a = a11, px0 @ p1x = p @ px2 -> PathSquare px0 px2 p p1x)
   with
   | 1 =>
       fun px2 : a00 = a11 =>
       match
         p1x as p in (_ = a)
         return
           (forall px3 : a00 = a, px0 @ p = 1 @ px3 -> PathSquare px0 px3 1 p)
       with
       | 1 =>
           fun (px3 : a00 = a10) (e : px0 @ 1 = 1 @ px3) =>
           (fun e' : px0 @ 1 = px3 =>
            match
              e' in (_ = p) return (px0 @ 1 = 1 @ p -> PathSquare px0 p 1 1)
            with
            | 1 =>
                fun e0 : px0 @ 1 = 1 @ (px0 @ 1) =>
                match
                  px0 as p in (_ = a)
                  return (p @ 1 = 1 @ (p @ 1) -> PathSquare p (p @ 1) 1 1)
                with
                | 1 => fun _ : 1 @ 1 = 1 @ (1 @ 1) => 1%square
                end e0
            end e) (e @ concat_1p px3)
       end px2
   end px1)

(fun X : PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x =>
 match
   X in (@PathSquare _ a001 a0 a002 a1 p p0 p1 p2) return (p @ p2 = p1 @ p0)
 with
 | @sq_id _ x => (fun x0 : A => 1) x
 end)
o match
    p0x as p in (_ = a)
    return
      (forall px2 : a = a11, px0 @ p1x = p @ px2 -> PathSquare px0 px2 p p1x)
  with
  | 1 =>
      fun px2 : a00 = a11 =>
      match
        p1x as p in (_ = a)
        return
          (forall px3 : a00 = a, px0 @ p = 1 @ px3 -> PathSquare px0 px3 1 p)
      with
      | 1 =>
          fun (px3 : a00 = a10) (e : px0 @ 1 = 1 @ px3) =>
          (fun e' : px0 @ 1 = px3 =>
           match
             e' in (_ = p) return (px0 @ 1 = 1 @ p -> PathSquare px0 p 1 1)
           with
           | 1 =>
               fun e0 : px0 @ 1 = 1 @ (px0 @ 1) =>
               match
                 px0 as p in (_ = a)
                 return (p @ 1 = 1 @ (p @ 1) -> PathSquare p (p @ 1) 1 1)
               with
               | 1 => fun _ : 1 @ 1 = 1 @ (1 @ 1) => 1%square
               end e0
           end e) (e @ concat_1p px3)
      end px2
  end px1 ==
idmap

(fun x : px0 @ 1 = 1 @ px1 =>
 match
   match
     x @ concat_1p px1 in (_ = p)
     return (px0 @ 1 = 1 @ p -> PathSquare px0 p 1 1)
   with
   | 1 =>
       fun e : px0 @ 1 = 1 @ (px0 @ 1) =>
       match
         px0 as p in (_ = a)
         return (p @ 1 = 1 @ (p @ 1) -> PathSquare p (p @ 1) 1 1)
       with
       | 1 => fun _ : 1 @ 1 = 1 @ (1 @ 1) => 1%square
       end e
   end x in (@PathSquare _ a001 a0 a01 a11 p p0 p1 p2)
   return (p @ p2 = p1 @ p0)
 with
 | @sq_id _ x0 => 1
 end) ==
idmap

match
  match
    e @ concat_1p px1 in (_ = p)
    return (px0 @ 1 = 1 @ p -> PathSquare px0 p 1 1)
  with
  | 1 =>
      fun e0 : px0 @ 1 = 1 @ (px0 @ 1) =>
      match
        px0 as p in (_ = a)
        return (p @ 1 = 1 @ (p @ 1) -> PathSquare p (p @ 1) 1 1)
      with
      | 1 => fun _ : 1 @ 1 = 1 @ (1 @ 1) => 1%square
      end e0
  end e in (@PathSquare _ a001 a0 a01 a11 p p0 p1 p2)
  return (p @ p2 = p1 @ p0)
with
| @sq_id _ x => 1
end = e

(fun p : px0 @ 1 = 1 @ px1 =>
 match
   match
     p @ concat_1p px1 in (_ = p0)
     return (px0 @ 1 = 1 @ p0 -> PathSquare px0 p0 1 1)
   with
   | 1 =>
       fun e0 : px0 @ 1 = 1 @ (px0 @ 1) =>
       match
         px0 as p0 in (_ = a)
         return (p0 @ 1 = 1 @ (p0 @ 1) -> PathSquare p0 (p0 @ 1) 1 1)
       with
       | 1 => fun _ : 1 @ 1 = 1 @ (1 @ 1) => 1%square
       end e0
   end p in (@PathSquare _ a001 a0 a01 a11 p0 p1 p2 p3)
   return (p0 @ p3 = p2 @ p1)
 with
 | @sq_id _ x => 1
 end = p) e

(fun p : px0 @ 1 = 1 @ px1 =>
 match
   match
     p @ concat_1p px1 in (_ = p0)
     return (px0 @ 1 = 1 @ p0 -> PathSquare px0 p0 1 1)
   with
   | 1 =>
       fun e0 : px0 @ 1 = 1 @ (px0 @ 1) =>
       match
         px0 as p0 in (_ = a)
         return (p0 @ 1 = 1 @ (p0 @ 1) -> PathSquare p0 (p0 @ 1) 1 1)
       with
       | 1 => fun _ : 1 @ 1 = 1 @ (1 @ 1) => 1%square
       end e0
   end p in (@PathSquare _ a001 a0 a01 a11 p0 p1 p2 p3)
   return (p0 @ p3 = p2 @ p1)
 with
 | @sq_id _ x => 1
 end = p) e

(fun p : px0 @ 1 = 1 @ px1 =>
 match
   match
     p @ concat_1p px1 in (_ = p0)
     return (px0 @ 1 = 1 @ p0 -> PathSquare px0 p0 1 1)
   with
   | 1 =>
       fun e0 : px0 @ 1 = 1 @ (px0 @ 1) =>
       match
         px0 as p0 in (_ = a)
         return (p0 @ 1 = 1 @ (p0 @ 1) -> PathSquare p0 (p0 @ 1) 1 1)
       with
       | 1 => fun _ : 1 @ 1 = 1 @ (1 @ 1) => 1%square
       end e0
   end p in (@PathSquare _ a001 a0 a01 a11 p0 p1 p2 p3)
   return (p0 @ p3 = p2 @ p1)
 with
 | @sq_id _ x => 1
 end = p) e
e'' = e

match
  match
    e'' @ concat_1p px1 in (_ = p)
    return (px0 @ 1 = 1 @ p -> PathSquare px0 p 1 1)
  with
  | 1 =>
      fun e0 : px0 @ 1 = 1 @ (px0 @ 1) =>
      match
        px0 as p in (_ = a)
        return (p @ 1 = 1 @ (p @ 1) -> PathSquare p (p @ 1) 1 1)
      with
      | 1 => fun _ : 1 @ 1 = 1 @ (1 @ 1) => 1%square
      end e0
  end e'' in (@PathSquare _ a001 a0 a01 a11 p p0 p1 p2)
  return (p @ p2 = p1 @ p0)
with
| @sq_id _ x => 1
end = e''
e'' = e

match
  match
    e'' @ concat_1p px1 in (_ = p)
    return (px0 @ 1 = 1 @ p -> PathSquare px0 p 1 1)
  with
  | 1 =>
      fun e0 : px0 @ 1 = 1 @ (px0 @ 1) =>
      match
        px0 as p in (_ = a)
        return (p @ 1 = 1 @ (p @ 1) -> PathSquare p (p @ 1) 1 1)
      with
      | 1 => fun _ : 1 @ 1 = 1 @ (1 @ 1) => 1%square
      end e0
  end e'' in (@PathSquare _ a001 a0 a01 a11 p p0 p1 p2)
  return (p @ p2 = p1 @ p0)
with
| @sq_id _ x => 1
end = e''

match
  match
    e'' @ concat_1p px1 in (_ = p)
    return (px0 @ 1 = 1 @ p -> PathSquare px0 p 1 1)
  with
  | 1 =>
      fun e : px0 @ 1 = 1 @ (px0 @ 1) =>
      match
        px0 as p in (_ = a)
        return (p @ 1 = 1 @ (p @ 1) -> PathSquare p (p @ 1) 1 1)
      with
      | 1 => fun _ : 1 @ 1 = 1 @ (1 @ 1) => 1%square
      end e
  end e'' in (@PathSquare _ a001 a0 a01 a11 p p0 p1 p2)
  return (p @ p2 = p1 @ p0)
with
| @sq_id _ x => 1
end = e''

match
  match
    e'' @ concat_1p (1 @ 1) in (_ = p)
    return (1 @ 1 = 1 @ p -> PathSquare 1 p 1 1)
  with
  | 1 => fun _ : 1 @ 1 = 1 @ (1 @ 1) => 1%square
  end e'' in (@PathSquare _ a001 a10 a01 a11 p p0 p1 p2)
  return (p @ p2 = p1 @ p0)
with
| @sq_id _ x => 1
end = e''
IsTrunc n (PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x)
IsTrunc n (PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x)

contents:
Definition equiv_sq_G1  : p = p' <~> PathSquare p p' 1 1
    := sq_path oE equiv_p1_1q.

Definition equiv_sq_1G : q = q' <~> PathSquare 1 1 q q'
    := sq_path oE equiv_1p_q1 oE equiv_path_inverse _ _.

Definition sq_refl_h {A} {a0 a1 : A} (p : a0 = a1)
  : PathSquare p p 1 1 := sq_G1 1.

Definition sq_refl_v {A} {a0 a1 : A} (p : a0 = a1)
  : PathSquare 1 1 p p := sq_1G 1.

goals.conclusion: PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x <~> PathSquare px1 px0 p0x^ p1x^
PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x <~> PathSquare px1 px0 p0x^ p1x^
PathSquare px0 px1 1 1 <~> PathSquare px1 px0 1^ 1^
PathSquare px0 px1 1 1 <~> px1 = px0
PathSquare px0 px1 1 1 <~> px0 = px1
PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x <~> PathSquare px0^ px1^ p1x p0x
PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x <~> PathSquare px0^ px1^ p1x p0x
PathSquare 1 1 p0x p1x <~> PathSquare 1^ 1^ p1x p0x
PathSquare 1 1 p0x p1x <~> p1x = p0x
PathSquare 1 1 p0x p1x <~> p0x = p1x

contents: (** Transpose of a square. We make a local definition that will never get unfolded. *)

goals.conclusion: PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x -> PathSquare p0x p1x px0 px1
PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x -> PathSquare p0x p1x px0 px1
PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x <~> PathSquare p0x p1x px0 px1
PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x <~> PathSquare p0x p1x px0 px1
tr o tr == idmap
tr o tr == idmap

contents: (** NOTE: sq_tr ought to be some sort of involution but it obviously isn't since it is not of the form A -> A. Perhaps there is a more general "involution" but between equivalent types? But then that very equivalence is given by sq_tr so it seems a bit circular... *)

Definition sq_tr_refl_h {A} {a b : A} {p : a = b}
  : sq_tr (sq_refl_h p) = sq_refl_v p.

goals.conclusion: sq_tr (sq_refl_h p) = sq_refl_v p
sq_tr (sq_refl_h p) = sq_refl_v p

contents:
Definition sq_tr_refl_v {A} {a b : A} {p : a = b}
  : sq_tr (sq_refl_v p) = sq_refl_h p.

goals.conclusion: sq_tr (sq_refl_v p) = sq_refl_h p
sq_tr (sq_refl_v p) = sq_refl_h p

contents: Definition equiv_sq_V : PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x <~> PathSquare px1^ px0^ p1x^ p0x^.

goals.conclusion: PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x <~> PathSquare px1^ px0^ p1x^ p0x^
PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x <~> PathSquare px1^ px0^ p1x^ p0x^
PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x <~> px1^ @ p0x^ = p1x^ @ px0^
PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x <~> (p0x @ px1)^ = (px0 @ p1x)^
PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x <~> p0x @ px1 = px0 @ p1x
PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x <~> PathSquare p0x p1x px0 px1

contents: (** Left rotation : left right top bottom  ->  top bottom right left. *)
Definition equiv_sq_rot_l : PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x <~> PathSquare p0x^ p1x^ px1 px0.

goals.conclusion: PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x <~> PathSquare p0x^ p1x^ px1 px0
PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x <~> PathSquare p0x^ p1x^ px1 px0
PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x <~> p0x^ @ px0 = px1 @ p1x^
PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x <~> px0 = p0x @ (px1 @ p1x^)
PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x <~> px0 = (p0x @ px1) @ p1x^
PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x <~> px0 @ p1x = p0x @ px1

contents: (** Right rotation : left right top bottom -> bottom top left right. *)
Definition equiv_sq_rot_r : PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x -> PathSquare p1x p0x px0^ px1^.

goals.conclusion: PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x -> PathSquare p1x p0x px0^ px1^
PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x -> PathSquare p1x p0x px0^ px1^
PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x <~> p1x @ px1^ = px0^ @ p0x
PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x <~> px0 @ (p1x @ px1^) = p0x
PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x <~> (px0 @ p1x) @ px1^ = p0x
PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x <~> px0 @ p1x = p0x @ px1

contents: (** Lemmas for rewriting sides of squares. *)
(** These are all special cases of the following "rewrite all sides" lemma which we prove is an equivalence giving us all special cases as equivalences too. *)

Definition equiv_sq_GGGG {px0' px1' p0x' p1x'} (qx0 : px0 = px0')
    (qx1 : px1 = px1') (q0x : p0x = p0x') (q1x : p1x = p1x')
    : PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x <~> PathSquare px0' px1' p0x' p1x'.

goals.conclusion: PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x <~> PathSquare px0' px1' p0x' p1x'
PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x <~> PathSquare px0' px1' p0x' p1x'

contents: by destruct qx0, qx1, q0x, q1x.
Definition equiv_sq_GGGc := equiv_sq_GGGG qx0 qx1 q0x 1.
Definition equiv_sq_cGGG := equiv_sq_GGGG 1 qx1 q0x q1x.

(** Operations to move paths around a square. We define all these operations immediately as equivalences. The naming first number indicates in which argument the path that moves is on the left of the equivalence, and the second number where it is on the right. The equivalences are all set up so that on the right, there is no path inversion. For the [24] and [13] equivalences there is a path inverse on the left. The corresponding equivalences [42] and [31] are the symmetric versions of these, but the path inverse is in another place. *)

Definition equiv_sq_move_23 {f12'' : x02 = x} {f12' : x = x22} 
    : PathSquare f10 (f12'' @ f12') f01 f21 <~> PathSquare f10 f12' (f01 @ f12'') f21.

goals.conclusion:

    = sq_ccGG (concat_p_pp _ _ _) (concat_p_pp _ _ _) (sq_concat_h s (sq_concat_h t u)).

goals.conclusion:
(s @@h t) @@h u =
sq_ccGG (concat_p_pp p0x p0y p0z) (concat_p_pp p1x p1y p1z) (s @@h (t @@h u))

contents: by destruct s, u, (sq_1G^-1 t), p0y.
(** [PathSquare] Kan fillers ~ Every open box has a lid. *)
(** These can be used to prove groupoid laws about paths. *)
Context {A : Type} {a00 a10 a01 a11 : A}.

Definition sq_fill_l (px1 : a01 = a11) (p0x : a00 = a01) (p1x : a10 = a11)
    : {px0 : a00 = a10 & PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x}.

goals.conclusion: {px0 : a00 = a10 & PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x}
{px0 : a00 = a10 & PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x}
PathSquare ((p0x @ px1) @ p1x^) px1 p0x p1x

contents:
Definition sq_fill_r (px0 : a00 = a10) (p0x : a00 = a01) (p1x : a10 = a11)
    : {px1 : a01 = a11 & PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x}.

goals.conclusion: {px1 : a01 = a11 & PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x}
{px1 : a01 = a11 & PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x}
PathSquare px0 ((p0x^ @ px0) @ p1x) p0x p1x

contents:
Definition sq_fill_t (px0 : a00 = a10) (px1 : a01 = a11) (p1x : a10 = a11)
    : {p0x : a00 = a01 & PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x}.

goals.conclusion: {p0x : a00 = a01 & PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x}
{p0x : a00 = a01 & PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x}
PathSquare px0 px1 ((px0 @ p1x) @ px1^) p1x

contents:
Definition sq_fill_b (px0 : a00 = a10) (px1 : a01 = a11) (p0x : a00 = a01)
    : {p1x : a10 = a11 & PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x}.

goals.conclusion: {p1x : a10 = a11 & PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x}
{p1x : a10 = a11 & PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x}
PathSquare px0 px1 p0x ((px0^ @ p0x) @ px1)

contents: (** To prove stronger uniqueness results and related induction principles, we need to start a new section so we can generalize over all of the points. *)
Context {A : Type} {a00 a10 a01 a11 : A}.

#[export] Instance sq_fill_l_contr (px1 : a01 = a11) (p0x : a00 = a01) (p1x : a10 = a11)
    : Contr {px0 : a00 = a10 & PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x}.

goals.conclusion: Contr {px0 : a00 = a10 & PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x}
Contr {px0 : a00 = a10 & PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x}

forall y : {px0 : a00 = a10 & PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x},
sq_fill_l px1 p0x p1x = y
sq_fill_l px1 p0x p1x = (px0'; s')

contents:
Definition sq_fill_l_uniq {px1 : a01 = a11} {p0x : a00 = a01} {p1x : a10 = a11}
    {px0 : a00 = a10} (s : PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x)
    {px0' : a00 = a10} (s' : PathSquare px0' px1 p0x p1x)
    : px0 = px0'
    := ap pr1 (@path_contr _ (sq_fill_l_contr px1 p0x p1x) (px0; s) (px0'; s')).

#[export] Instance sq_fill_r_contr (px0 : a00 = a10) (p0x : a00 = a01) (p1x : a10 = a11)
    : Contr {px1 : a01 = a11 & PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x}.

goals.conclusion: Contr {px1 : a01 = a11 & PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x}
Contr {px1 : a01 = a11 & PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x}

forall y : {px1 : a01 = a11 & PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x},
sq_fill_r px0 p0x p1x = y
sq_fill_r px0 p0x p1x = (px1'; s')

contents:
Definition sq_fill_r_uniq {px0 : a00 = a10} {p0x : a00 = a01} {p1x : a10 = a11}
    {px1 : a01 = a11} (s : PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x)
    {px1' : a01 = a11} (s' : PathSquare px0 px1' p0x p1x)
    : px1 = px1'
    := ap pr1 (@path_contr _ (sq_fill_r_contr px0 p0x p1x) (px1; s) (px1'; s')).

#[export] Instance sq_fill_t_contr (px0 : a00 = a10) (px1 : a01 = a11) (p1x : a10 = a11)
    : Contr {p0x : a00 = a01 & PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x}.

goals.conclusion: Contr {p0x : a00 = a01 & PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x}
Contr {p0x : a00 = a01 & PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x}

forall y : {p0x : a00 = a01 & PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x},
sq_fill_t px0 px1 p1x = y
sq_fill_t px0 px1 p1x = (p0x'; s')

contents:
Definition sq_fill_t_uniq {px0 : a00 = a10} {px1 : a01 = a11} {p1x : a10 = a11}
    {p0x : a00 = a01} (s : PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x)
    {p0x' : a00 = a01} (s' : PathSquare px0 px1 p0x' p1x)
    : p0x = p0x'
    := ap pr1 (@path_contr _ (sq_fill_t_contr px0 px1 p1x) (p0x; s) (p0x'; s')).

#[export] Instance sq_fill_b_contr (px0 : a00 = a10) (px1 : a01 = a11) (p0x : a00 = a01)
    : Contr {p1x : a10 = a11 & PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x}.

goals.conclusion: Contr {p1x : a10 = a11 & PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x}
Contr {p1x : a10 = a11 & PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x}

forall y : {p1x : a10 = a11 & PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x},
sq_fill_b px0 px1 p0x = y
sq_fill_b px0 px1 p0x = (p1x'; s')

contents:
Definition sq_fill_b_uniq {px0 : a00 = a10} {px1 : a01 = a11} {p0x : a00 = a01}
    {p1x : a10 = a11} (s : PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x)
    {p1x' : a10 = a11} (s' : PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x')
    : p1x = p1x'
    := ap pr1 (@path_contr _ (sq_fill_b_contr px0 px1 p0x) (p1x; s) (p1x'; s')).

(** Induction principles that only require one edge to be free.  It might be possible to use these to simplify other work involving squares, including work earlier in the file, so we could consider moving these and the material on fillers earlier in the file. *)

Definition pathsquare_ind_l
                      {px1 : a01 = a11}
    {p0x : a00 = a01} {p1x : a10 = a11}
    (P : forall (px0 : a00 = a10) (sq : PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x), Type)
    (fill := (sq_fill_l px1 p0x p1x))
    (p : P fill.1 fill.2)
    : forall px0 sq, P px0 sq.

goals.conclusion: forall (px0 : a00 = a10) (sq : PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x), P px0 sq

hypotheses.type: forall px0 : a00 = a10, PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x -> Type
{px0 : a00 = a10 & PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x}

goals.conclusion: forall (px0 : a00 = a10) (sq : PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x), P px0 sq

hypotheses.type: forall px0 : a00 = a10, PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x -> Type
{px0 : a00 = a10 & PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x}

goals.conclusion: P px0 sq

hypotheses.type: forall px2 : a00 = a10, PathSquare px2 px1 p0x p1x -> Type
{px2 : a00 = a10 & PathSquare px2 px1 p0x p1x}

    {px0 : a00 = a10}
    {p0x : a00 = a01} {p1x : a10 = a11}
    (P : forall (px1 : a01 = a11) (sq : PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x), Type)

Definition pathsquare_ind_t
    {px0 : a00 = a10} {px1 : a01 = a11}
                      {p1x : a10 = a11}

contents:
(** An interchange law between horizontal and vertical composites in the following square:
<<
            h0i          h0j
       a00 ━━━━━━━━ a01 ━━━━━━━━ a02
        ┃            ┃            ┃
        ┃  sq00      ┃  sq01      ┃
    vi0 ┃        vi1 ┃            ┃ vi2
        ┃            ┃            ┃
        ┃   h1i      ┃   h1j      ┃
       a10 ━━━━━━━━ a11 ━━━━━━━━ a12
        ┃            ┃            ┃
        ┃  sq10      ┃  sq11      ┃
    vj0 ┃        vj1 ┃            ┃ vj2
        ┃            ┃            ┃
        ┃   h2i      ┃   h2j      ┃
       a20 ━━━━━━━━ a21 ━━━━━━━━ a22
>>
*)

Definition sq_interchange {A}
  {a00 a10 a20 a01 a11 a21 a02 a12 a22 : A} (* 9 points in big square of 2x2 squares *)
  (* sq00, top left, 4 paths *)
  (vi0 : a00 = a10) (* i = 0,1 *)
  (h0i : a00 = a01)
  (vi1 : a01 = a11)
  (h1i : a10 = a11)
  (* sq10, bottom left, 3 paths, h1i shared with sq00 *)
  (vj0 : a10 = a20) (* j = 1,2 *)
  (vj1 : a11 = a21)
  (h2i : a20 = a21)
  (* sq01, top right, 3 paths, vi1 shared with sq00 *)
  (vi2 : a02 = a12)
  (h0j : a01 = a02)
  (h1j : a11 = a12)
  (* sq11, bottom right, 2 paths, vj1 shared with sq10 and h1j with sq01 *)
  (vj2 : a12 = a22)
  (h2j : a21 = a22)
  (sq00 : PathSquare vi0 vi1 h0i h1i)
  (sq10 : PathSquare vj0 vj1 h1i h2i)
  (sq01 : PathSquare vi1 vi2 h0j h1j)
  (sq11 : PathSquare vj1 vj2 h1j h2j)
  : (sq00 @@v sq10) @@h (sq01 @@v sq11) = (sq00 @@h sq01) @@v (sq10 @@h sq11).

goals.conclusion:
(1%square @@v sq10) @@h (sq01 @@v 1%square) =
(1%square @@h sq01) @@v (sq10 @@h 1%square)

(1%square @@v sq10) @@h (sq01 @@v 1%square) =
(1%square @@h sq01) @@v (sq10 @@h 1%square)

(1%square @@v (sq_fill_l 1 1 1).2) @@h (sq01 @@v 1%square) =
(1%square @@h sq01) @@v ((sq_fill_l 1 1 1).2 @@h 1%square)

contents:
(** Squares arising from [ap]. *)

(** Apply a function to the sides of square. *)

goals.conclusion:
PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x ->
PathSquare (ap f px0) (ap f px1) (ap f p0x) (ap f p1x)

PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x ->
PathSquare (ap f px0) (ap f px1) (ap f p0x) (ap f p1x)

contents:
Definition sq_ap_refl_h {A B} (f : A -> B) {a0 a1 : A} (p : a0 = a1)
  : sq_ap f (sq_refl_h p) = sq_refl_h (ap f p).

goals.conclusion: sq_ap f (sq_refl_h p) = sq_refl_h (ap f p)
sq_ap f (sq_refl_h p) = sq_refl_h (ap f p)

contents:
Definition sq_ap_refl_v {A B} (f : A -> B) {a0 a1 : A} (p : a0 = a1)
  : sq_ap f (sq_refl_v p) = sq_refl_v (ap f p).

goals.conclusion: sq_ap f (sq_refl_v p) = sq_refl_v (ap f p)
sq_ap f (sq_refl_v p) = sq_refl_v (ap f p)

contents:
Definition ap_nat {A B} {f f' : A -> B} (h : f == f') {x y : A} (p : x = y)
  : PathSquare (ap f p) (ap f' p) (h x) (h y).

goals.conclusion: PathSquare (ap f p) (ap f' p) (h x) (h y)
PathSquare (ap f p) (ap f' p) (h x) (h y)

contents: (** The transpose of the naturality square. *)

Definition ap_nat' {A B} {f f' : A -> B} (h : f == f') {x y : A} (p : x = y)
  : PathSquare (h x) (h y) (ap f p) (ap f' p).

goals.conclusion: PathSquare (h x) (h y) (ap f p) (ap f' p)
PathSquare (h x) (h y) (ap f p) (ap f' p)

contents:
Definition ap_compose_sq {A B C} (f : A -> B) (g : B -> C) {x y : A} (p : x = y)
  : PathSquare (ap (g o f) p) (ap g (ap f p)) 1 1 := sq_G1 (ap_compose f g p).

Definition ap_idmap_sq {A} {x y : A} (p : x = y) : PathSquare (ap idmap p) p 1 1
  := sq_G1 (ap_idmap p).
(** A [DPath] of a certain form can be turned into a square. *)

goals.conclusion: DPath (fun x : A => f x = g x) p q1 q2 <~> PathSquare q1 q2 (ap f p) (ap g p)
DPath (fun x : A => f x = g x) p q1 q2 <~> PathSquare q1 q2 (ap f p) (ap g p)
DPath (fun x : A => f x = g x) 1 q1 q2 <~> PathSquare q1 q2 (ap f 1) (ap g 1)

contents:
Definition sq_ap011 {A B C} (f : A -> B -> C)
  {a a' : A} (p : a = a') {b b' : B} (q : b = b')
  : PathSquare (ap (fun x => f x b) p) (ap (fun x => f x b') p)
    (ap (f a) q) (ap (f a') q).

goals.conclusion:
PathSquare (ap (fun x : A => f x b) p) (ap (fun x : A => f x b') p)
  (ap (f a) q) (ap (f a') q)

PathSquare (ap (fun x : A => f x b) p) (ap (fun x : A => f x b') p)
  (ap (f a) q) (ap (f a') q)

DPath (fun x : B => f a x = f a' x) q (ap (fun x : A => f x b) p)
  (ap (fun x : A => f x b') p)

contents: exact (apD (fun y => ap (fun x => f x y) p) q).

goals.conclusion:
PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x * PathSquare qx0 qx1 q0x q1x <~>
PathSquare (path_prod' px0 qx0) (path_prod' px1 qx1) 
  (path_prod' p0x q0x) (path_prod' p1x q1x)

PathSquare px0 px1 p0x p1x * PathSquare qx0 qx1 q0x q1x <~>
PathSquare (path_prod' px0 qx0) (path_prod' px1 qx1) 
  (path_prod' p0x q0x) (path_prod' p1x q1x)

(px0 @ p1x = p0x @ px1) * (qx0 @ q1x = q0x @ qx1) <~>
PathSquare (path_prod' px0 qx0) (path_prod' px1 qx1) 
  (path_prod' p0x q0x) (path_prod' p1x q1x)

(px0 @ p1x, qx0 @ q1x) = (p0x @ px1, q0x @ qx1) <~>
PathSquare (path_prod' px0 qx0) (path_prod' px1 qx1) 
  (path_prod' p0x q0x) (path_prod' p1x q1x)

?f (px0 @ p1x, qx0 @ q1x) = ?f (p0x @ px1, q0x @ qx1) <~>
PathSquare (path_prod' px0 qx0) (path_prod' px1 qx1) 
  (path_prod' p0x q0x) (path_prod' p1x q1x)
Type

equiv_path_prod (a00, b00) (a11, b11) (px0 @ p1x, qx0 @ q1x) =
equiv_path_prod (a00, b00) (a11, b11) (p0x @ px1, q0x @ qx1) <~>
PathSquare (path_prod' px0 qx0) (path_prod' px1 qx1) 
  (path_prod' p0x q0x) (path_prod' p1x q1x)

?Goal0 = equiv_path_prod (a00, b00) (a11, b11) (p0x @ px1, q0x @ qx1) <~>
PathSquare (path_prod' px0 qx0) (path_prod' px1 qx1) 
  (path_prod' p0x q0x) (path_prod' p1x q1x)
equiv_path_prod (a00, b00) (a11, b11) (px0 @ p1x, qx0 @ q1x) = ?Goal0

path_prod (a00, b00) (a10, b10) px0 qx0 @
path_prod (a10, b10) (a11, b11) p1x q1x =
equiv_path_prod (a00, b00) (a11, b11) (p0x @ px1, q0x @ qx1) <~>
PathSquare (path_prod' px0 qx0) (path_prod' px1 qx1) 
  (path_prod' p0x q0x) (path_prod' p1x q1x)

path_prod (a00, b00) (a10, b10) px0 qx0 @
path_prod (a10, b10) (a11, b11) p1x q1x = ?Goal0 <~>
PathSquare (path_prod' px0 qx0) (path_prod' px1 qx1) 
  (path_prod' p0x q0x) (path_prod' p1x q1x)
equiv_path_prod (a00, b00) (a11, b11) (p0x @ px1, q0x @ qx1) = ?Goal0

path_prod (a00, b00) (a10, b10) px0 qx0 @
path_prod (a10, b10) (a11, b11) p1x q1x =
path_prod (a00, b00) (a01, b01) p0x q0x @
path_prod (a01, b01) (a11, b11) px1 qx1 <~>
PathSquare (path_prod' px0 qx0) (path_prod' px1 qx1) 
  (path_prod' p0x q0x) (path_prod' p1x q1x)
